# diagramkit

> Diagram rendering CLI & library. Converts .mermaid, .excalidraw, .drawio, and Graphviz .dot/.gv/.graphviz files to SVG/PNG/JPEG/WebP/AVIF with automatic light/dark mode support.

## Quick Start

```bash
npm add diagramkit
npx diagramkit warmup
npx diagramkit render .
```

## Supported Formats

Input: .mermaid (.mmd, .mmdc), .excalidraw, .drawio (.drawio.xml, .dio), .dot (.gv, .graphviz)
Output: SVG (default), PNG, JPEG, WebP, AVIF

Prefer SVG unless the destination explicitly needs raster (email, Confluence).

## CLI

```
diagramkit render <file-or-dir> [options]    Render diagram file(s) to images
diagramkit <file-or-dir> [options]           Alias for "diagramkit render"
diagramkit warmup                            Pre-install Playwright chromium
diagramkit doctor                            Validate runtime dependencies
diagramkit init [--ts]                       Create config file
diagramkit --install-skill                   Install project skills for Claude and Cursor
diagramkit --version / --help / --agent-help
```

### Render Options

```
--format <formats>       Output formats, comma-separated (default: svg)
--theme <light|dark|both> Theme variants (default: both)
--scale <number>         Scale for raster output (default: 2)
--quality <number>       JPEG/WebP/AVIF quality 1-100 (default: 90)
--force                  Re-render all, ignore manifest
--watch                  Watch for changes and re-render
--no-contrast            Disable dark SVG contrast optimization
--type <type>            Filter: mermaid | excalidraw | drawio | graphviz
--output <dir>           Custom output directory
--config <path>          Path to config file (skip auto-discovery)
--output-dir <name>      Output folder name (default: .diagramkit)
--manifest-file <name>   Manifest filename (default: manifest.json)
--no-manifest            Disable manifest tracking
--same-folder            Output next to source files
--output-prefix <string> Prefix for output filenames (default: '')
--output-suffix <string> Suffix for output filenames (default: '')
--dry-run                Preview what would render
--plan                   Show stale plan with reasons (directory mode)
--quiet                  Suppress info output
--json                   Machine-readable JSON output
--log-level <level>      Logging verbosity (silent/error/warn/info/verbose)
--strict-config          Fail on config warnings/invalid values
--max-type-lanes <1-4>   Limit concurrent engine lanes
```

### Examples

```bash
diagramkit render .                                  # All diagrams in cwd
diagramkit render flow.mermaid                       # Single file
diagramkit render . --format svg,png                 # Multi-format
diagramkit render ./docs --format png --scale 3      # High-res PNGs
diagramkit render . --watch                          # Watch mode
diagramkit render . --output ./build/images          # Custom output dir
diagramkit render . --type mermaid --force           # Force mermaid only
diagramkit render . --config ./custom.config.json5   # Explicit config
```

## Output Convention

Rendered images go to `.diagramkit/` next to source files:

```
docs/
  architecture.mermaid
  .diagramkit/
    architecture-light.svg
    architecture-dark.svg
    manifest.json
```

## Configuration

Config file: `diagramkit.config.json5` or `diagramkit.config.ts`
Global: `~/.config/diagramkit/config.json5`
Merge order: defaults → global → env vars (DIAGRAMKIT_*) → local → CLI flags

Key options: outputDir, manifestFile, useManifest, sameFolder, defaultFormats, defaultTheme, outputPrefix, outputSuffix, extensionMap, overrides (per-file settings).

Per-file overrides example:
```json5
{
  overrides: {
    "hero.mermaid": { formats: ["svg", "png"], scale: 3 },
    "docs/*.excalidraw": { theme: "light" },
  },
}
```

## Dependencies

All rendering engines (mermaid, excalidraw, draw.io, graphviz) are bundled.
Optional: `sharp` (for PNG/JPEG/WebP/AVIF raster output)

## Agent Files

All three files are shipped in npm:

- `llms-quick.txt` - shortest command-oriented guide
- `llms.txt` - practical defaults and CLI usage
- `llms-full.txt` - complete CLI + API + type reference

Use `diagramkit --agent-help` when you want the full reference in one command output.

## Repo Bootstrap With Agents

Use `node_modules/diagramkit/llms.txt` as the first file an agent reads after installing the package.

Recommended repo bootstrap steps:

- add `"render:diagrams": "diagramkit render ."` to `package.json`
- create `diagramkit.config.json5` only when the repository needs non-default behavior
- run `npx diagramkit --install-skill` to create `.claude/skills/diagramkit/SKILL.md` and `.cursor/skills/diagramkit/SKILL.md`
- run `npx diagramkit warmup` unless the repository is Graphviz-only

## Programmatic API

```ts
import { render, renderFile, renderAll, watchDiagrams, loadConfig, warmup, dispose } from 'diagramkit'
```

`--json` returns a versioned envelope (`schemaVersion: 1`) with nested `result`.
JSON schema: `schemas/diagramkit-cli-render.v1.json` (shipped in npm package).

Exit codes: `0` = success, `1` = error (render failure, unknown command, etc.).
Use `--quiet --json` together for clean JSON on stdout (suppresses log noise).

<!-- pagesmith-ai:shared-llms:start -->
# Pagesmith

> Pagesmith is a filesystem-first content toolkit with `@pagesmith/core` and `@pagesmith/docs`.

## @pagesmith/core — Content Layer

Schema-validated content collections, lazy markdown rendering (Expressive Code syntax highlighting), JSX runtime, CSS exports, and Vite plugins.

### Basic Setup (Vite Plugin)

```ts
import { createContentLayer, defineCollection, defineConfig, z } from '@pagesmith/core'

const posts = defineCollection({
  loader: 'markdown',
  directory: 'content/posts',
  schema: z.object({
    title: z.string(),
    description: z.string().optional(),
    date: z.coerce.date(),
    tags: z.array(z.string()).default([]),
  }),
})

const layer = createContentLayer(
  defineConfig({
    collections: { posts },
  }),
)

const entries = await layer.getCollection('posts')
const rendered = await entries[0]?.render()
```

### Vite Integration

```ts
import { pagesmithContent, pagesmithSsg } from '@pagesmith/core/vite'
import collections from './content.config'

export default defineConfig({
  plugins: [
    pagesmithContent({ collections }),
    pagesmithSsg({ entry: './src/entry-server.tsx' }),
  ],
})
```

Import collections as virtual modules: `import posts from 'virtual:content/posts'`

## @pagesmith/docs — Documentation Sites

Convention-based docs with default theme, Pagefind search, sidebar generation, and layout overrides.

### Basic Setup

```json5
// pagesmith.config.json5
{
  name: 'Acme Docs',
  title: 'Acme Docs',
  description: 'Multi-package documentation',
  contentDir: './content',
  outDir: './dist',
  footerLinks: [
    { label: 'Guide', path: '/guide' },
    { label: 'Reference', path: '/reference' },
  ],
  search: { enabled: true },
}
```

```text
content/
  README.md                 # Home page (DocHome layout)
  guide/
    meta.json5              # Section ordering
    getting-started/
      README.md             # A page
  reference/
    api/README.md
```

### Layout Overrides

```json5
{
  theme: {
    layouts: {
      home: './theme/layouts/DocHome.tsx',
      page: './theme/layouts/DocPage.tsx',
      notFound: './theme/layouts/DocNotFound.tsx',
    },
  },
}
```

### CLI

```bash
pagesmith init --ai  # Initialize config + content + AI integrations
pagesmith dev        # Development server
pagesmith build      # Production build
pagesmith preview    # Preview built site
pagesmith mcp --stdio # Start MCP server for AI agents
```
<!-- pagesmith-ai:shared-llms:end -->
